summary: Phase 2A Expenses List frontend testing on web preview. PASSED: screen renders with 5 seeded transactions, dynamic count, search ('Net' -> 1, clear -> 5), category single-select filter (Food -> 1), payment single-select filter (UPI -> 2), category+payment AND combine (Food+UPI -> 1), date headers (YESTERDAY, TUE 16 JUN, etc.), FAB present, empty state for 'zzzzz'. CRITICAL FAILURE: tapping the FAB (or any edit action) crashes the Add Expense screen with 'ReferenceError: editingId is not defined' at /app/frontend/app/add-expense.tsx:359:16 — useLocalSearchParams is imported but never called, and `editingId` is referenced on lines 207 and 359 without being declared. This regresses Phase 1 (Add Expense from FAB) AND blocks the new Phase 2A Edit flow entirely. Sort feature also appears not to reorder rows visually in the test run; could not re-verify after the FAB crash because the LogBox red error overlay covered interactions. Swipe-gesture testIDs (delete-<id>, edit-<id>) are present in DOM but force-clicking them via Playwright does not fire onPress (renderRightActions/renderLeftActions are hidden offscreen) — confirmed environment limitation per the request; should be verified manually or on native.

ui_bugs.issue: ReferenceError: editingId is not defined. `useLocalSearchParams` is imported (line 15) but never called; `editingId` is referenced on lines 207 (`if (editingId) await updateExpense(editingId, payload)`) and 359 (`{editingId ? 'Update Expense' : 'Save Expense'}`). Render crashes the Add Expense screen the moment the FAB is tapped. This blocks BOTH adding a new expense (Phase 1 regression) AND editing via the swipe-right edit action (Phase 2A). Reference: /app/frontend/app/add-expense.tsx:95-100, 207, 359.

After tapping sort-button -> sort-option-highest, the visual order of rows did not change (Zomato/Yesterday still appeared first instead of DMart ₹1,250). Same observation for sort-option-oldest. Sort label in the row updates (modal closes correctly) but list ordering may not be re-applied. NOTE: could not re-verify after FAB crash dialog blocked the UI; main agent should reproduce after fixing the editingId crash. Reference: /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/expenses.tsx:108-119.

integration_issues.flow: FAB -> Add Expense (Phase 1) AND swipe-edit -> Add Expense (Phase 2A)
integration_issues.issue: Both flows crash on the new Add Expense screen render due to missing `editingId` declaration. No new expenses can be added and no expense can be edited via the UI.

Browser automation cannot simulate native pan gestures; the renderRightActions/renderLeftActions in ReanimatedSwipeable are not interactable via force-click in Playwright (clicking the delete-<id> element does nothing — count stays at 5, undo-toast never appears). Per the request this is a non-blocking environment limitation. Recommend manual verification on iOS/Android or via Expo Go for delete + undo behavior.

react_native_issues.issue: Console warning: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents'. The Animated.View check overlay at line 385 passes pointerEvents as a prop instead of via style. Reference: /app/frontend/app/add-expense.tsx:385.

Console warning: '"shadow*" style props are deprecated. Use "boxShadow".' Affects components using shadowColor/shadowOffset/shadowOpacity/shadowRadius on web. Non-blocking but should be migrated to boxShadow on web.

action_items: FIX CRITICAL: In /app/frontend/app/add-expense.tsx, call useLocalSearchParams() and derive editingId, e.g. `const params = useLocalSearchParams<{ id?: string; title?: string; amount?: string; category?: string; payment_method?: string; expense_date?: string; expense_time?: string; note?: string; }>(); const editingId = params.id ? Number(params.id) : null;`. Then prefill state in a useEffect from params (merchant, expr, category, payment, when, note) when editingId is set so the edit flow shows the existing values. Without this fix BOTH Add and Edit flows crash.

Investigate Sort: confirm that selecting sort-option-highest / sort-option-oldest actually re-renders the FlatList in the new order. Code at /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/expenses.tsx:108-119 looks correct, so this may be a rerender/key issue or related to FlatList keying; re-test after fixing editingId.
Replace deprecated `pointerEvents` prop with `style={{ pointerEvents: 'none' }}` at /app/frontend/app/add-expense.tsx:385.
(Optional) Migrate shadow* style props to boxShadow for web compatibility to silence the deprecation warning.
Manually verify swipe-to-delete (with 4s undo toast) and swipe-to-edit on a native device / Expo Go since Playwright cannot simulate pan gestures.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/frontend/app/add-expense.tsx references `editingId` without declaring it — production runtime ReferenceError. useLocalSearchParams is imported but never used.

/app/frontend/app/add-expense.tsx does not prefill any of merchant/expr/category/payment/when/note from route params, so even after fixing editingId the edit flow will not display the expense's existing values (only the button label flips to 'Update Expense').

(tabs)/expenses.tsx — pending delete is committed on unmount/blur via useFocusEffect cleanup. This is a reasonable approach, but if the user backgrounds the app within the 4s undo window on native the delete will still fire — acceptable, just worth flagging.

success_rate.frontend: ~55% (6 of 8 features pass; FAB+edit Add flow broken, sort unverified)

seed_data_creation: None — used the 5 seeded expenses already created by db.ts on first launch.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Re-run after main agent declares editingId and adds param-driven prefill in /app/frontend/app/add-expense.tsx. Verify: (1) tapping add-fab opens Add Expense and Save Expense saves a new row (count 5 -> 6). (2) tapping edit-<id> opens Add Expense with all fields prefilled and button text 'Update Expense'; changing amount and tapping it updates the SAME row (count unchanged). (3) sort-option-highest reorders rows so DMart ₹1,250 is first; sort-option-oldest reverses order. Swipe-based delete/undo cannot be exercised in browser — note as env limitation. Onboarding flow each fresh session: Get Started -> name 'Tester' -> Continue -> Skip for now -> Expenses tab.
rca_of_issue: Root cause: /app/frontend/app/add-expense.tsx was edited to add edit-mode support but only the consumer references were added (`editingId` on lines 207, 359 and the import of useLocalSearchParams on line 15). The actual declaration (`const params = useLocalSearchParams(); const editingId = params.id ? Number(params.id) : null;`) and the param-driven state prefill (useEffect setting expr, merchant, category, payment, when, note) were never committed. Reproduction: launch web preview -> complete onboarding -> tap Expenses tab -> tap floating + FAB (testID add-fab). The router navigates to /add-expense which throws `ReferenceError: editingId is not defined` at line 359 during the JSX render of the Save button. Mitigation: add the declaration + prefill block at the top of AddExpense before any JSX referencing editingId is evaluated.
